name: To the city of Berja — from Adra
name_original: Hasta la ciudad de Berja — desde Adra
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Adra offer a diverse landscape, transitioning from coastal plains to the foothills of the Sierra Nevada. The region features a varied terrain with both flat coastal stretches and significant elevation gains inland. Cyclists can expect routes that follow the coastline, providing views of beaches and cliffs, as well as paths that ascend into the hills towards areas like Berja. This combination of coastal and mountainous proximity defines the cycling experience in Adra. Best road cycling routes around Adra The most popular road cycling route is To the city of Berja — from Adra, a 27.9 miles (44.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 10 minutes to complete. This moderate route includes a steady ascent towards the inland town of Berja. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Atalaya del Cautor Tower – Playa de Casarones loop from El Lance de la Virgen, a difficult 50.5 miles (81.3 km) path. This route features substantial climbs and coastal views, passing landmarks like the Atalaya del Cautor Tower. Local road cyclists also love the Adra (Torre de los Perdigones) – Berja loop from El Lance de la Virgen, a 39.3 miles (63.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant elevation, often completed in about 3 hours 2 minutes. Road cycling around Adra is defined by scenic coastal rides, challenging climbs into the hills, and routes offering views of the Mediterranean Sea. The network provides options for different ability levels, from moderate coastal loops to difficult mountain ascents. The routes in Adra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 15 reviews. More than 800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Adra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Adra?
A: Adra offers a diverse selection of over 20 road cycling routes. These routes cater to various skill levels, ranging from easy coastal rides to challenging mountain ascents into the foothills of the Sierra Nevada.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Adra's road cycling routes?
A: You can expect a varied terrain in Adra. Routes transition from flat coastal plains with scenic views of beaches and cliffs to significant elevation gains as you head inland towards areas like Berja. The region offers both well-paved coastal stretches and more demanding climbs into the hills.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Adra?
A: Yes, Adra has options for beginners. While many routes involve elevation, there is at least one easy route available. Shorter, well-paved loops, such as those along the Beach Promenade, are generally suitable for less experienced cyclists or those looking for a relaxed ride.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes in Adra?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes extending towards areas like Benínar, Turón, and La Parra offer significant elevation gains, some exceeding 3,000 feet. An example of a difficult route with substantial climbs and coastal views is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31819294" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atalaya del Cautor Tower – Playa de Casarones loop from El Lance de la Virgen&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Adra?
A: Adra benefits from a warm Mediterranean climate, making it suitable for year-round outdoor activities. However, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer while still enjoying mild weather.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Adra?
A: Yes, many of Adra's road cycling routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1284036" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Adra (Torre de los Perdigones) – Berja loop from El Lance de la Virgen&lt;/a&gt; offers a varied circular ride through the region.

Q: What landmarks or viewpoints can I see along Adra's road cycling routes?
A: Adra's routes offer various scenic points. You might encounter historical elements and natural features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31819294" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atalaya del Cautor Tower – Playa de Casarones loop&lt;/a&gt; passes the Atalaya del Cautor Tower, known for its beautiful views of the Costa Tropical. Other notable attractions in the wider area include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/744872" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guardias Viejas Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5098686" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port of Adra&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes in Adra?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations are not always clear, shorter, well-paved coastal loops are generally suitable for families with children. Routes with minimal elevation gain and away from heavy traffic would be the best choice for a family outing.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Adra?
A: The komoot community highly rates road cycling in Adra, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 15 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the combination of scenic coastal rides with challenging climbs into the hills, and the rewarding views of the Mediterranean Sea.

Q: Are there routes that offer both coastal views and inland exploration?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Adra are characterized by this blend. You can start with coastal stretches offering views of the sea and then ascend into the hills, transitioning to greener landscapes and more demanding climbs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984370735" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;To the city of Berja — from Adra&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, takes you inland towards the town of Berja after starting near the coast.

Q: Are there any traffic-free road cycling options in Adra?
A: While most road cycling routes share roads with vehicles, some segments, particularly near places like Playa de Casarones, are noted for offering beautiful, traffic-free road rides. Additionally, dedicated cycle paths like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5470445" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coastal Road from La Rábita to Adra&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4430381" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike Path from Balerma to Guardias Viejas&lt;/a&gt; provide safer, more relaxed options.

Q: What is the longest road cycling route available in Adra?
A: One of the longer and more challenging routes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31819294" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atalaya del Cautor Tower – Playa de Casarones loop from El Lance de la Virgen&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 50 miles (81.3 km) and features significant elevation changes.
tips: Road that goes in the direction of El Carmen and that climbs through the Río Chico valley in the direction of the town of Berja.

Berja is a town located in the southwest of the Sierra de Gádor and where it is surrounded by mountains despite being very close to the sea. The Sierra Alhamilla makes it difficult to see the sea, where the well-known "sea of plastic" begins.

It does not go unnoticed when one approaches Adra. It looks on the horizon. It serves as a reference point. It is the Tower of the Pellets, the emblem of the city of Adra. It was built for the manufacture of pellets
